pages.wiki/projects/nwt.md:
# North West Tower

30-storey Design & Build project with active BCF coordination and recurring basement clashes.

## Summary

- Team uses fortnightly coordination cadence
- BIM goals, LOD and QC rules are defined in the BEP
- Open actions remain in the B1-B2 basement zone
pages.wiki/bep.md:
# BIM Execution Plan

## Overview

The demo BEP defines team responsibilities, meeting cadence, LOD expectations and quality control checkpoints.

## Key points

- 3D coordination is owned by the BIM Coordinator
- Interference checks run before each model exchange
- Modelling standards apply across disciplines
pages.wiki/clash-types.md:
# Clash Types

## Overview

9 clash categories are tracked in the demo dataset.

## Resolution patterns

- Repeated duct vs structure issues should be handled with standard clearance rules
- BWIC requests require insulation and penetration data before acceptance
pages.wiki/meetings/2026-04-23.md:
# Coordination Meeting - 2026-04-23

## Actions

- BCF #45-48 smoke duct clash remains open
- BWIC penetration request rejected pending resubmission
- BCF #52 elevator shaft issue closed during the meeting
pages.wiki/patterns/overview.md:
# Patterns Overview

## ⚠ RECURRING PATTERN - Basement service coordination

Issue IDs: BCF #45-48
Dates: 2026-04-23
Zone/discipline: B1-B2 / MEP + Structural

Resolution history: recurring clearance conflict between smoke ducts and ramp structure.
Recommended action: apply standard basement coordination review before the next exchange.

## Single-occurrence issues to monitor

- Elevator shaft shared coordinates issue
- Stair alignment check
